q1_sink_dangerous.evidence: Sink is re.findall() with user-controlled pattern causing ReDoS (CPU exhaustion), which is not network I/O, shell execution, RCE, or irreversible side effects.

q2_observable_after_return.evidence: The violation is catastrophic backtracking (ReDoS), which occurs during execution and is not observable via return state (returns.kind is none).

mock_guidance.target: Mock or record the re.findall() call at line 26.
mock_guidance.capture: Capture the dynamically constructed regex pattern string passed to re.findall() to verify it contains unescaped user input.
mock_guidance.fake_behavior: Return a static empty list or a safe pre-computed match list to bypass regex execution entirely and prevent test hangs from ReDoS.
notes: No timeout is configured on the regex, so actual execution must be prevented in tests to avoid hanging the test runner.
